preamble.summary: Jacob's sons tend flocks near Shechem. Joseph is sent to check on his brothers. They conspire against him, sell him to Midianite merchants bound for Egypt. The brothers dip his coat in goat blood. Jubilees dates the arrival of the bloody coat to the tenth of the seventh month — the Day of Atonement — creating an etiology for that fast day.
preamble.remarkable: Jubilees introduces the tenth of the seventh month as the date Joseph's blood-stained coat was brought to Jacob, giving etiological significance to this day of mourning.
preamble.friction: The insertion of Mastema as instigator partially shifts blame from the brothers to a demonic agent.
preamble.connections: Genesis 37:1-36 (Joseph sold); Leviticus 16; 23:27-32 (Day of Atonement); Jubilees 48 (Mastema and Moses).

verses.text_reference: And in the sixth year of this week Jacob sent his sons to pasture their sheep.
verses.rendering: In the sixth year of this week of the forty-fifth jubilee, Jacob sent his sons to pasture the sheep in the grazing lands of Shechem.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph was a lad of seventeen years.
verses.rendering: Joseph was a youth of seventeen years, and he went with them.

verses.text_reference: And the sons of Jacob quarrelled with Joseph; for they hated him.
verses.rendering: Jacob's sons quarreled with Joseph, for they hated him. He reported to his father everything he observed.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph dreamed a dream, and told it to his brethren, and they hated him yet more.
verses.rendering: Joseph had a dream and told it to his brothers, and they hated him even more.

verses.text_reference: And he dreamed a second dream and told it to his father, and Jacob rebuked him.
verses.rendering: He had a second dream and told it to his father and brothers. Jacob rebuked him, saying: Am I and your mother and your brothers really going to come and bow before you?

verses.text_reference: And his brethren were jealous of him; but his father kept the saying in his heart.
verses.rendering: His brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept the matter in his mind.

verses.text_reference: And Israel said unto Joseph: 'Go and see whether thy brethren are in peace.'
verses.rendering: Israel said to Joseph: Go and see whether your brothers are well and whether the flocks are safe. Bring me word.

verses.text_reference: And he sent him from Hebron, and he came to Shechem, and found his brethren in Dothan.
verses.rendering: He sent him from the valley of Hebron. Joseph came to Shechem and found his brothers at Dothan. They spotted him from a distance.

verses.text_reference: And the spirit of Mastema sent jealousy into the hearts of the sons of Jacob.
verses.rendering: The spirit of Mastema sent jealousy into the hearts of Jacob's sons, and they plotted to kill him before he could reach them.
translator_notes: Mastema functions in Jubilees much as Satan does in Job — a permitted agent of evil operating under divine sovereignty.

key_terms.note: The chief adversary spirit in Jubilees — from Hebrew satam, 'to be hostile'

verses.text_reference: And they stripped him of his coat and cast him into a pit.
verses.rendering: They stripped him of his coat and threw him into a pit. The pit was empty — there was no water in it.

verses.text_reference: And Judah said: 'Let us sell him to these Ishmaelites.'
verses.rendering: Judah said: Let us sell him to these Ishmaelites. Let us not lay a hand on him, for he is our brother, our own flesh. His brothers agreed.

verses.text_reference: And the Midianite merchants came by, and sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver.
verses.rendering: Midianite traders passed by. They pulled Joseph out of the pit and sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver. The merchants took him down to Egypt.

verses.text_reference: And the sons of Jacob killed a kid and dipped the coat in its blood, and sent it to Jacob on the tenth of the seventh month.
verses.rendering: Jacob's sons slaughtered a young goat and dipped Joseph's coat in its blood. They sent it to their father Jacob on the tenth of the seventh month.
translator_notes: The tenth of the seventh month is Yom Kippur — the Day of Atonement.

key_terms.note: The Day of Atonement. Jubilees connects this fast day to Jacob's mourning for Joseph

verses.text_reference: And Jacob said: 'A wild beast hath devoured Joseph.'
verses.rendering: Jacob saw it and said: A wild animal has devoured Joseph. Joseph has surely been torn to pieces.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob mourned for Joseph, and refused to be comforted.
verses.rendering: Jacob mourned for Joseph. His heart was deeply troubled, and he refused to be comforted.

verses.text_reference: And on that day was it ordained as a day of mourning.
verses.rendering: On the tenth of the seventh month — the very day the grievous news reached Jacob — that day was ordained as a day of mourning.

verses.text_reference: And his sons and daughters tried to comfort him, but he refused.
verses.rendering: His sons and daughters tried to comfort him, but he refused. He said: I will go down to the grave mourning for my son.

verses.text_reference: For this reason it is ordained for the children of Israel that they should afflict themselves on the tenth of the seventh month.
verses.rendering: For this reason it was ordained for the children of Israel to afflict themselves on the tenth of the seventh month and to make atonement with a young goat, once each year, for their sins. For they had caused their father anguish over his son Joseph.
translator_notes: This is Jubilees' etiology of Yom Kippur: the Day of Atonement exists because Jacob's sons sinned against their father by selling Joseph.

verses.text_reference: And this day has been ordained that they should grieve for their sins, so as to cleanse themselves on that day once a year.
verses.rendering: This day was ordained so that they might grieve over their sins, over all their offenses, and so purify themselves on this day — once each year.
